title: File I/O error messages swallowed to String via map_err(|e| e.to_string()) — stringly-typed errors
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Error Orchestration
Location: src/tui/app.rs:214-220 save_session returns Result<(), String>; AppEvent::ApiError(String) at src/tui/events.rs:43; multiple places in tui/app.rs use .unwrap_or_else(|e| format!(...))
Violation: Throughout the TUI seam, structured errors (io::Error, serde_json::Error, reqwest::Error) are eagerly flattened to String. This loses: error source chain (io::Error::kind), actionable discrimination (NotFound vs PermissionDenied), and prevents programmatic handling. The TUI displays the string, but the pattern also propagates up: AppEvent::ApiError(String) means the event loop cannot distinguish network errors from invalid-responses. Conflicts with project policy (CLAUDE.md: 'Use thiserror for library errors with structured variants').
Mandated Refactor: Define pub enum TuiError in src/tui/error.rs with #[derive(thiserror::Error)] variants { SessionIo(#[from] io::Error), SessionParse(#[from] serde_json::Error), Api(#[from] ApiError), HookBlocked { reason: String } }. Replace String payloads on AppEvent::ApiError with AppEvent::Error(TuiError). Only stringify at the render boundary.
